coredata.dc:title: Clustering of carbon quantum dots from polysaccharides (Cellulose, Alginate, Chitosan) versus heterocyclic compounds: Synthesis, characterization and medical applications

coredata.dc:description: Carbon quantum dots are special class of carbon nanostructures with spherical size of one to ten nanometers. Synthesis techniques of carbon quantum dots are diverse from top-down to bottom-up techniques but most of them are complicated, energy consuming, time-consuming and expensive. However, the hydrothermal technique has advantages of high yield, easy control and simplicity than other synthesis methods. Due to high availability of carbon precursors, biocompatibility, high surface area, low cost, high photostability and low cytotoxicity properties of carbon quantum dots, they have diverse medical applications which are currently summarized in the presented review. The current review represents an overview for the compatibility and superiority of polysaccharides (e.g. pectin, alginate, cellulose, chitosan) versus heterocyclic compounds for successive clustering of carbon quantum dots.

Techniques of synthesis of carbon quantum dots (CQDs).

Described mechanism of utilization of CQDs in engineering of bone tissue.

Anti-inflammatory action of CQDs; [a] in mice via comparison among, [i] control, [ii] ibuprofen and [iii] ibuprofen-CQDs (IOP Publishing right access; Number 1510067–1) (Qu et al., 2020). [b] in human (RAW264.7 cells), the relation between CQDs concentration and the NO levels (Elsevier right access; Number 5815510769571 (Lu et al., 2024).

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Carbon quantum dots are special class of carbon nanostructures with spherical size of one to ten nanometers. Synthesis techniques of carbon quantum dots are diverse from top-down to bottom-up techniques but most of them are complicated, energy consuming, time-consuming and expensive. However, the hydrothermal technique has advantages of high yield, easy control and simplicity than other synthesis methods. Due to high availability of carbon precursors, biocompatibility, high surface area, low cost, high photostability and low cytotoxicity properties of carbon quantum dots, they have diverse medical applications which are currently summarized in the presented review. The current review represents an overview for the compatibility and superiority of polysaccharides (e.g. pectin, alginate, cellulose, chitosan) versus heterocyclic compounds for successive clustering of carbon quantum dots.

ce:keyword.ce:text: Heterocyclic compounds as carbon quantum dots reactants
Medical applications of carbon quantum dots

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Nanotechnology is widely used for various medical purposes in recent years due to the high effectiveness and competence of nanostructured materials. Nanoparticles (NPs) have small size that below one hundred nanometers that acquire them superior optical, mechanical and magnetic characteristics. Optical characteristics of NPs originates from their specific surface plasmon resonance (SPR) that leads to the estimation of absorption at certain wavelength for each type of NPs. SPR initiates when the frequency of incident photons appropriates with the excitation of surface electrons of NPs that leads to nearly specific UV–Visible SPR peak for each type of NPs. The mechanical characteristics of NPs as strain, elasticity, hardness, adhesion, and stress were found to be different from the micro-size of the same particles that is utilized in nanocomposite coatings and additives. Due to presence of free electrons on the surface of NPs, the magnetic field is initiated (Khan, Saeed & Khan, 2019). The characteristics of NPs depend on their size and shape where NPs have diverse shapes as spherical, tetrahedral (Zahmatkesh et al., 2021), cubic, nanotubes, nanowires (Khodashenas & Ghorbani, 2019), triangular (Li et al., 2012), etc.

The progress in nanotechnology resulted in generation of new NPs that can be used in therapeutic applications. Because of high surface area, very small size and SPR of NPs, they have high reactivity so, they have high chance to be used in several medical applications as anti-cancer (Khursheed et al., 2022), anti-Alzheimer, anti-inflammatory (Al-Radadi et al., 2022), anti-viral (Alavi, Kamarasu, McClements & Moore, 2022), anti-oxidant, anti-bacterial, bio-imaging (Khursheed et al., 2022; Singh & Amiji, 2022; Tejwan et al., 2022), biosensors (Khursheed et al., 2022), anti-microbial reactivity (Olga et al., 2022), drug delivery, diagnosis and therapy of cardiovascular disease (Khursheed et al., 2022; Li et al., 2022b; Zheng, Zhu, Lv, Gu & Hu, 2022), sensing (Wang, Wu, Xing & Wang, 2022), diabetic medicine (Li et al., 2022) and medical textiles that used in hospitals like in wound healing, bed covers and curtains (Ahmed et al., 2021; Barman et al., 2022).

Nanostructured materials can be classified into two types according to the precursors. The two types may be are metallic precursors which are metal salts and non-metallic precursors which are carbon sources. Metallic precursors are used for synthesis of metallic nanoparticles (MNPs) for example: silver nitrate (AgNO3) for silver nanoparticles AgNPs, gold chloride (AuCl3) for gold nanoparticles AuNPs, palladium chloride (PdCl2) for palladium nanoparticles PdNPs, etc.(Ahmed, Mikhail, El-Sherbiny, Nagy & Emam, 2020; Emam, Mikhail, El-Sherbiny, Nagy & Ahmed, 2020). Non-metallic precursors or carbon precursors are used for synthesis of carbon nanostructures as carbon nanotubes, nano-diamonds, and recently, carbon quantum dots (CQDs) (Henna et al., 2020). Whereas, CQDs is unique member of carbon nanostructures with particle size 1–10 nm, spherical in shape and exhibit superior optical characters, to be cosiderably applicable in different biomedical and environmental purposes.

Despite of several medical applications of MNPs are been existed, the toxicity of MNPs must be considered. Due to the small size of MNPs, they can be absorbed through human body and reached to any organ causing generation of species of reactive oxygen that causes changing of gene composition and inducing apoptotic pathways. Some articles showed that the MNPs can be accumulated in brain, liver, kidney, heart, and lung in vivo tests of mice (Zinicovscaia et al., 2018) and zebra fish (d'Amora et al., 2022) where MNPs can induce generation of lactate dehydrogenase which increases reactive oxygen percent that causes various diseases. These effects represent risks to the man health (Li et al., 2022). Also, it is found that the MNPs are toxic to microalgae (Mahana, Guliy & Mehta, 2021). On the other hand, CQDs are more safe and biocompatible than MNPs so, CQDs can be easily got out from the organisms through urine (Truskewycz et al., 2022). In recent years, CQDs acquire great importance due to availability of carbon in many precursors around us. CQDs were synthesized from numerous sources or precursors which can be classified to natural precursors and synthetic precursors. Natural precursors may be like coal, grass, various plant parts as leaves, stalk, roots and fruits but each of them consists of several compounds together which results in indefinite structure of CQDs and wide range of particle size. On the other hand, CQDs can be synthesized from different types of specific polysaccharides that result in definite shape of CQDs and synthetic precursors as synthetic heterocyclic compounds that contain carbon, hydrogen and other heteroatoms like nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur (Emam, El-Shahat, Hasanin & Ahmed, 2021; Kurian & Paul, 2021).

Carbon is unparalleled element that can form many several structures. There is very high percentage of known compounds that contain carbon in their structures. This is because of presence of four valence electrons in 2s and 2p energy levels which facilitates formation of different types of bonds as single, double and triple bonds that can make sp3, sp2 and sp hybridization forms, respectively, which make carbon to be the important element in industries and materials science (Slepičková Kasálková, Slepička & Švorčík, 2021). The nanoparticles that depend on carbon have promising properties such as low toxicity, high surface area, very high availability of carbon sources, stable fluorescence, in addition to advantageous biological, physical and chemical properties which acquire carbon nanoparticles high importance and become an attractive field of research in recent years.

There are different allotropes of carbon nanostructures due to different arrangements of carbon atoms in addition to various sizes and shapes of carbon nanostructures like zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures that mean the size of all dimensions of the carbon nanostructures under nanometer range as fullerenes & carbon dots. One-dimensional carbon nanostructures One-dimensional (1D) nanostructures, are commonly defined as linear structures with diameter <100 nm as carbon single walled nanotubes, carbon multi walled nanotubes and carbon nanohorns (Adul-Rasool et al., 2024). Two-dimensional carbon nanostructures that mean the size of only two dimensions of the carbon nanostructures is lower than 10 nanometers as carbon nanoribbons, unzipped nanotubes of carbon and graphene. Three-dimensional carbon nanostructures that mean the size of all three dimensions is higher than 10 nanometers as diamond, graphite and multilayered sheets of graphite (Henna et al., 2020) as in Fig. 1 . It is clear that zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures are better than one-dimensional, two-dimensional and three-dimensional carbon nanostructures due to the smallest size of all dimensions of zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures. Fullerene has ball-shape structure consisting of sixty carbon atoms with diameter 0.7 nm. Fullerene has disadvantages as aggregation in different aqueous mediums due to poor solubility of fullerene (Henna et al., 2020). Onions-like carbon composed of closed shells of carbon that resemble to onions which makes them to have poor solubility so; carbon dots are the best choice for researching in recent years.

Graphene is one layer consisting of carbon atoms in hexagons shape with sp2 hybridization where one carbon atom connects to three carbon atoms through σ bonds while perpendicular non-hybridized P z orbital forms π bond with unhybridized P z orbital of another carbon. The σ bonds in graphene are shorter than that of diamond where this acquires graphene higher mechanical strength. The π bonds that are out of the σ bonds plane allow electron delocalization providing high electrical conductivity. Graphene can be synthesized by both bottom up and top-down processes. Bottom-up processes like chemical vapor deposition, growth on silicon carbide and chemical vapor deposition improved by plasma. Top-down processes like chemical exfoliation, mechanical exfoliation and graphene oxide reduction.

There are derivatives of graphene like graphene oxide and the opposite derivative; reduced graphene oxide. The graphene oxide is graphene with functional groups containing oxygen on their structures as a result of oxidation while reduced graphene oxide is graphene oxide with reduction process that reset graphene structure with remaining some oxygen atoms. Graphene was utilized in many applications as bioimaging, biosensors, tissue engineering and drug delivery. To reduce toxicity of graphene, surface functionalization was investigated by covalent binding via chemical reaction between functional groups of graphene and biomolecules. On the other hand, surface functionalization was investigated by non-covalent binding through biomolecules adsorption via force of Van der Waals (Liu & Speranza, 2019). Usually, graphene has size more than one hundred nanometers while nano-graphene has one of its dimensions is less than one hundred nanometers (Wang, Narita & Müllen, 2017). Graphene is considered as two-dimensional carbon nanostructures where the size of two dimensions is lower than 10 nanometers so, it is important to search about zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures to have lower size and higher reactivity.

Graphite consists of carbon atoms with sp2 hybridization in layers’ form that are bonded with forces of Van der Waals that make it more flexible and softer so, graphite is utilized as lubricant in industries. Graphite has size which is less than one millimeter. Graphite was synthesized by reduction without solvent (Li et al., 2022). Graphite is considered as three-dimensional carbon nanostructures where the size of all three dimensions is higher than 10 nanometers so, it is important to search about zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures to have lower size and higher reactivity.

Carbon nanotubes are hollow cylindrical graphene sheets with sp2 hybridization. They may be carbon nanotubes having single wall with length in range of twenty to one thousand nanometers or carbon nanotubes having multi walls with length in range of one to five hundred micrometers. Carbon nanotubes were synthesized via laser ablation, chemical vapor deposition and arc discharge. In Laser ablation method, graphite and metallic catalyst are placed in quartz tube and entered into furnace then laser beam is emitted to the quartz tube in presence of inert gas after that, evaporation of catalyst and graphite is occurred following by re-condensation which results in production of carbon nanotubes accompanied with impurities. This laser ablation method consumes high energy and is cost consumable. In chemical vapor deposition, hydrocarbons like acetylene, methane, ethylene, ethane, or alcohols and metallic catalyst, as Co, Ni or Fe can be entered to furnace at 1000 °C where hydrocarbon molecules are degraded to active carbons that are adsorbed on the catalyst surface then carbon nanotubes were precipitated. In arc discharge process, the anode is synthesized from metal catalyst and graphite; on the other hand, the cathode is prepared from graphite. In presence of helium gas and when arc current is started, evaporation and condensation of anode are occurred on the surface of cathode to synthesis of carbon nanotubes with single walls and multi walls accompanied with impurities. Carbon nanotubes have diverse application fields as in environment, energy, electronics and nanomedicine like drug delivery where they can be loaded by drugs in internal and external wall surface in addition to ability to penetrate the cell wall of the aimed cells due to their one - dimensional structure which results to high uptake so, carbon nanotubes have high efficiency in decreasing tumor growth and have no toxic effect for normal cells. Also, carbon nanotubes are utilized in biosensors where carbon nanotubes can improve electron transfer between an analyte and a sensor acceptor so, it increases the sensor sensitivity. In addition to tissue engineering and bioimaging (Liu & Speranza, 2019). Carbon nanotubes are considered as one-dimensional carbon nanostructures where the size of one dimension is lower than 10 nanometers so, it is important to search about zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures to have lower size and higher reactivity.

Carbon fibers are fibers consisting of carbon atoms in shape of graphitic structure. Firstly, they were synthesized from cellulose by carbonization at elevated temperature. Recently, carbon fibers are synthesized from rayon, petroleum pitch and polyacrylonitrile. They can be utilized in aerospace industries due to their good mechanical properties and lightness. Also, carbon fibers are used in military and civil engineering fields because of good thermal, electrical and optical properties. In addition to that, carbon fibers can be combined with metals, plastic and ceramic to form composites with low density, heat resistance, high strength and high stability. In biomedicine, carbon fibers are used due to their chemical inertness, biocompatibility and high stability in vivo tests. Also, carbon fibers are utilized in tissue repairing and improving tissue regeneration. Carbon fibers can be combined with polymers like epoxy resins, polyethylene, polyamide, etc. for enhancing mechanical properties of polymers to be biocompatible for bones. Also, carbon fibers are utilized in wound healing and bone repairing due to their flexibility and improved mechanical properties. Carbon fibers that synthesized from polyacrylonitrile are used in production of composites that are utilized in aerospace, automotive industry and sporting goods (Liu & Speranza, 2019). Particle size of carbon fibers is in micrometer scale, so, it is important to search about zero-dimensional carbon nanostructures to have lower size and higher reactivity.

Carbon dots (CDs) are considered as novel structures that are type of carbon nanostructures which opened an interesting new field of research. CDs were discovered at the beginning of the 21st century where CDs were known during the synthesis of carbon nanotubes with single wall where the resulted impurities were short tubular carbon and fluorescent carbon (Xu et al., 2004). The expression CDs was appeared for first time when nanocarbons were produced from carbon source via laser ablation where the surface of the produced CDs was passivated by polyethylene glycol that leads to very low quantum yield (QY) which is 10 %, in addition to that, CDs have round shape with size in range from one to ten nanometers (Sun et al., 2006).

CDs structure is proposed to consist of a core containing sp2/sp3 carbon bonds with an amorphous surface containing numerous polymer chains or functional groups that contain nitrogen or oxygen (Zhai et al., 2022). CDs may be classified into four categories as carbon quantum dots (CQDs), graphene quantum dots (GQDs), carbonized polymer dots (CPDs) and carbon nanodots (CNDs) as in Fig. 1 (Zhai et al., 2022). CQDs have graphitic layers and surface functional groups with spherical shape while GQDs consist of one graphene layer with edges of functional groups. CPDs are hybrid of carbon and polymer with low degree of carbonization in addition to functional groups and polymer chains on the surface while CNDs consist of amorphous carbon core in spherical shape with functional groups on the surface (Zhai et al., 2022).

Single CQD unit consists of carbon core and surface groups containing nitrogen or oxygen atoms. The carbon core contains sp2 or sp3 hybridized carbon or connected benzene rings and heterocyclic compounds containing heteroatoms as nitrogen, oxygen, or sulfur and functional groups (Mintz et al., 2021). CQDs are zero dimensional spherical nanocarbons having size 1 to 10 nanometers with promising properties as stability in many solvents as water, dimethylformamide (DMF), etc. since CQDs have many surface groups that enhance the dispersibility and stability of CQDs (He, Xu, Zhang & Long, 2022; Yan et al., 2019). Recently, CQDs were synthesized from different sources and applied in many applications as bioimaging, determination of Fe (III) ions in human serum samples and tap water (Arvapalli, Sheardy, Alapati & Wei, 2020). The chemical composition, physical and optical properties of CQDs can be investigated via different instrumental analysis such as UV‐VIS spectroscopy, fluorescence spectrophotometry, IR (infra-red spectroscopy), XRD (x-ray diffraction), XPS (X-ray photon spectroscopy), Raman spectroscopy, HRTEM (high resolution transmission electron microscope)..etc.., in order to be applicable in various purposes (Batool, Haider & Javed, 2022).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis techniques of carbon quantum dots
ce:section.ce:para: The synthetic technique plays very important role in controlling size, properties, and applications of carbon quantum dots (CQDs). The techniques of CQDs synthesis can be described in two ways as top-down technique and bottom-up technique as in Fig. 2 .

Top-down technique includes degradation of large bulks of carbon material such as graphite oxide, graphene, carbon powder and carbon nanotubes to smaller parts or fragments of CQDs via electrochemical methods (Deng et al., 2014), laser ablation (Sun et al., 2006), arc discharge (Xu et al., 2004), chemical oxidation (Zhang et al., 2022) and pyrolysis.

Electrochemical method means that there are reactions of oxidation and reduction that are occurred at moderate temperature and pressure. It contains electrode and electrolyte to produce CQDs where the electrode can be graphite rod or carbon fiber. This electrolytic cell has potential difference among the two electrodes and the current passing through the electrolytic cell can be considered as index for CQDs properties (Rocco, Moldoveanu, Feroci, Bortolami & Vetica, 2023). The concentration of electrolyte solution and pH can affect the size of resulted CQDs. By controlling the electrolyte contents and redox reaction, the CQDs surface can be functionalized with different functional groups as hydroxyl, carboxyl and amino groups. CQDs were synthesized from graphite electrode in solution of sodium dihydrogen phosphate. Also, CQDs were synthesized by oxidation from graphite electrode in solution of potassium-sodium tartrate (Zhang et al., 2022) but this method is complicated, uses non-renewable carbon precursors and energy consumable so, it is important to search about more simple synthetic technique.

In laser ablation, laser pulses with high energy are directed toward carbon precursors that results in high temperature and pressure. The generated heat makes plasma which forms steam then by crystallization method, the steam results in CQDs formation. CQDs were synthesized from cement and graphite as carbon precursors via laser ablation then nitric acid was used for CQDs surface passivation which converts un-luminescent CQDs to luminescent CQDs when exposed to excitation wavelength that depends on size distribution of CQDs. In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from carbon sources and organic solvents where the luminescence characteristics can be controlled by utilizing of different organic solvents (Zhang et al., 2022). It is showed that this method consumes high energy, time and complicated equipment's so, it is important to search about more simple synthetic technique for CQDs formation.

In which carbon source is decomposed to smaller fragments as free radicals at anodic electrode through gas plasma in a reactor that results in recrystallization of carbon fragments to make electric arc at cathodic electrode. Via condensing of vapor carbon, nanocarbons are formed. The nanocarbons are extracted by oxidizing agents solution (Thakur et al., 2023). CQDs were firstly discovered via arc discharge of carbon source that synthesized carbon nanotubes with single wall where the resulted impurities contained carbon dots which are extracted via centrifugation. The fluorescence of resulted carbon dots were appeared when excited via 365 nm wavelength (Xu et al., 2004). In arc discharge method, there is anode, cathode and helium gas where by arc current, the anode is evaporated and condensed to form CQDs with impurities (Zhang et al., 2022). It is clear that, this method requires complicated equipment and high energy so, it is important to search about more simple synthetic technique for CQDs formation.

Chemical oxidation includes burning carbon precursors in acid oxide. CQDs were synthesized from 3-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-L-alanine with and without solvent via oxidation technique where the resulted CQDs that were synthesized with nitric acid solvent had size of 4.31 nm and QY of 1 % while the resulted CQDs that were synthesized without solvent exhibited with size of 3.64 nm and the QY increased to 6.3 % (Xu et al., 2013). Also, CQDs were synthesized from carbon fibers via heating with concentrated acid but this method has low efficiency and low QY (Zhang et al., 2022), in addition to that, this method use toxic chemicals and complicated equipment so, it is important to search about more simple synthetic technique for CQDs synthesis. Table 1 described the top-down techniques for CQDs synthesis.

Bottom-up technique includes building up nano-sized CQDs ranged between one and ten nanometers from smaller heterocyclic or biopolymer precursors in molecular or atomic level via re-polymerization then aromatization stages by hydrothermal or solvothermal method (Wang et al., 2022), microwave method (Tejwan et al., 2022), thermal decomposition (Park & Yang, 2022) and recently infrared-assisted technique (Ahmed et al., 2021).

In hydrothermal method, CQDs are synthesized from carbon precursors and water as solvent in hydrothermal autoclave cup at high diverse pressures and temperatures in furnace. In this method, CQDs are synthesized through the stages of re-polymerization and re-carbonization of carbon precursor for different time intervals and temperature degrees. For example, CQDs with QY of 2.72 % and size of 4 nm were synthesized from graphite oxide within nitric acid and water solvent via hydrothermal method (Wang et al., 2011). Poly-ethylene glycol was utilized in CQDs synthesis with size of 5 nm via hydrothermal technique within distilled water solvent with sodium hydroxide where the synthesized CQDs were used for detection of Hg2+(Liu et al., 2013).

In another approach, CQDs as nano-sensors with QY of 38.7 % and size of 4.8 nm were synthesized from ethanol within deionized water in addition to hydrogen peroxide via hydrothermal technique (Hu, Yang, Jia & Yu, 2015). CQDs with size of 6.41 nm were synthesized from carbon precursor like chocolate via hydrothermal technique where the emission peak of fluorescence depends on the wavelength of excitation. CQDs were synthesized from m-phenylenediamine via hydrothermal technique with QY of 12 % and size of 5.1 nm. The synthesized CQDs were utilized as drug carriers and antimicrobial reagents (Saravanan et al., 2020). Polylactide pellets were utilized in CQDs synthesis via hydrothermal technique (Lauria & Lizundia, 2020).

Also, citric acid and phenylalanine were exploited for CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 2 to 3 nm via hydrothermal technique for sensing of Fe3+(Pu et al., 2020). Polyvinyl alcohol and citric acid were utilized in CQDs synthesis via hydrothermal technique with size ranged from 2 to 4 nm for sensing purposes (Luo et al., 2021). Also, ammonium sulfate was utilized for CQDs synthesis via hydrothermal method with size of 5 nm (Bisauriya et al., 2022). Some researchers utilized carbon precursors containing carboxylic acid and amino functional groups that increase QY of CQDs via hydrothermal technique like CQDs that were synthesized with QY of 26 % and size in range of 1.5 – 4.5 nm from carbon precursor like orange juice via hydrothermal process (Zhang et al., 2022).

Solvothermal method depends on hydrothermal technique which involves reaction of nonaqueous or organic solvent instead of water with carbon precursors in the autoclave cup through a furnace at different time intervals and temperatures. For example, in presence of sodium hydroxide, polyethylene glycol was utilized in CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 1 to 5 nm via solvothermal technique (Mitra et al., 2013). Also, ethanolamine was utilized in CQDs synthesis with and without hydrogen peroxide solvent with size of 8.3 and 2.7 nm respectively, in addition to that; by using hydrogen peroxide solvent, QY increased from 7 % to 10.3 % (Dong et al., 2014). 3-Bromophenol was utilized in CQDs synthesis within deionized water and ethanol solvents via solvothermal method that resulted in QY of 19.6 % and size of 5.2 nm where the resulted CQDs were applied for bioimaging (Zou et al., 2016).

In another research, CQDs with size ranged from 3 to 8 nm and QY of 12.5 % were synthesized from n-methyl ethanol ammonium thioglycolate in hydrogen peroxide solvent in addition to water via solvothermal-ultrasonic method where the synthesized CQDs were applied for pesticides detection (Li et al., 2016). O-phenylenediamine was utilized in CQDs synthesis within ethanol solvent via solvothermal method where the formed CQDs had QY of 20 % and size ranged from 1 to 2 nm that used for atrazine detection (Mohapatra, Bera & Das, 2018).O-phenylenediamine, 1, 2, 4-benzenetriamine dihydrochloride and 1, 2, 4, 5-benzenetetramine tetrahydrochloride were separately utilized in CQDs synthesis within ethanol solvent via solvothermal technique which resulted the size of 6.50, 8.60 and 9.39 nm respectively (D. Zhao et al., 2019). Also, dihydrate form of copper (II) chloride within ethane diamine solvent were utilized in CQDs synthesis via solvothermal technique with QY of 7.8 % and size of 1.8 nm (Fang, Zhuo & Zhu, 2019).

Urea, dimethylformamide and diammonium citrate were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size 5.2 nm via solvothermal technique (Mandal & Das, 2020). In addition, 1,5-diaminonaphthalene, citric acid and ethanol solvent were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size 9.6 nm for antibacterial purposes (Nie et al., 2020). In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from nitro-benzaldehyde and glutaraldehyde in separate form within ethanol solvent via solvothermal method that resulted in size of 5 and 1 nm respectively for bioimaging purposes (Mohammadi, Mohammadi & Salimi, 2021).

O-phenylene diamine and seleno-urea were utilized in CQDs synthesis in hydrochloric acid solvent via solvothermal method with QY of 23.6 % and size of 3 nm for malachite detection in tissue of fish (Hu, Gao & Luo, 2021). Diphenyl ether within p-phenylenediamine solvent were utilized for CQDs synthesis with QY of 8 %, size of 2.8 nm and photoluminescence emission in ultraviolet region (Liu, Zhao, Shi & Lü, 2021). O-phenylenediamine within ethanol solvent via solvothermal method was utilized in CQDs synthesis with size of 5 nm (Bisauriya et al., 2022). In another research, o-phenylenediamine and m-phenylenediamine were separately utilized in synthesis of CQDs within ethanol solvent via solvothermal method that resulted in CQDs with size of 7.8 and 6.9 nm, respectively (Zhu, Han, Liu & Sun, 2022). In addition, CQDs were synthesized from diethylenetriamine and citric acid in diverse solvents with multiple concentrations via solvothermal technique (Zhang et al., 2022). These methods are considered to be effective since they can use renewable carbon precursors, non-toxic chemicals and simple equipment. Also, this method has advantages of high yield, easy control, lower toxic pollution and simple equipment than other synthesis methods.

In this technique, high frequency of electromagnetic field is directed toward the sample causing the motion of its molecules to be increased which elevates the temperature of the sample continuously to change the composition of the original sample by re-carbonization via breaking bonds to start generation of CQDs. For example, CQDs with QY of 2.72 % and size of 4 nm were synthesized from graphite oxide within nitric acid solvent via microwave method (Wang et al., 2011). Poly-ethylene glycol was utilized in CQDs synthesis via microwave pyrolysis within glycerin and serine solvents where the synthesized CQDs were used for cobalt sensing (Shi, Lu, Yan & Ma, 2013). Ammonia and aspartic acid were utilized in CQDs synthesis via microwave method for anti-counterfeiting applications (Hu et al., 2020). Citric acid and ethylenediamine were utilized in CQDs with size of 7 nm via microwave method while by using of citric acid and urea as precursors, the size of CQDs decreased to 2.4 nm for Fe3+ sensing (Arvapalli et al., 2020). Glycerol, citric acid and ethylenediamine were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of range from 2 to 8 nm via microwave method (Mai, Chi, Nguyen & Ta, 2020). In another article, CQDs were synthesized from sucrose with diethylene glycol for one minute in microwave oven (Zhang et al., 2022). The disadvantages of this method are using microwave radiation that causes great changes in the structure of the carbon precursors in addition to requirement of high energy and specific equipment.

In thermal decomposition, CQDs are synthesized via carbonizing large amount of carbon precursor without solvent at high temperature. The different fluorescence performance is due to different utilized temperature and time. It is found that by using higher temperature, carbonization of CQDs increases but fluorescence decreases. For example, polyethylene glycol was utilized in CQDs synthesis with QY of 16 % and size of 10 nm via thermal decomposition (Gogoi & Chowdhury, 2020). CQDs are synthesized from citric acid at 200 °C for 20 min via thermal decomposition (Zhang et al., 2022). This method has disadvantage of requiring complicated equipment and large amounts of carbon precursors.

Pyrolysis means heating of powder precursors of CQDs at different temperatures that exceed 500 ºC for various time intervals in the absence of oxygen. The disadvantages of this method are presence of many steps for CQDs purification and time consuming. There are researches that used pyrolysis for CQDs synthesis from m-phenylenediamine, p-phenylenediamine, citric acid monohydrate that are utilized in cephalosporin antibiotics detection in addition to cell imaging. In another research, CQDs with size which was not exceed 5 nm, were synthesized via pyrolysis from grinded algae where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in applications of energy storage as described in Table 2 .

In infrared-assisted technique, CQDs are synthesized through exposing the carbon precursor to infra-red irradiation. For example, CQDs with size of 8 nm were synthesized from carboxymethyl cellulose with sodium hydroxide via infrared synthetic method (Ahmed et al., 2021). It is clear that, this method requires complicated equipment and high energy. Table 2 described the bottom-up techniques for CQDs synthesis. It is concluded that the top-down techniques consume higher energy, longer time and lower efficiency than bottom-up techniques for CQDs synthesis.

The applications of CQDs are affected by their photoluminescence (PL) where PL is the emitted light wavelength from the sample when it absorbs appropriate light wavelength that causes excitation of electrons to higher energy levels then after some time, the excited electrons lose photons of energy in two shapes and return to the original energy levels (Li et al., 2010; Sk, Ananthanarayanan, Huang, Lim & Chen, 2014; Xingchen, Wenhui & Chenxin, 2021). The two shapes of PL are: phosphorescence and fluorescence where phosphorescence is PL that remains for long time after light absorption and excitation or in another meaning higher life time of decaying since the excited electrons are transferred from excited singlet state to triplet state via intersystem crossing then are returned to ground state releasing energy which is called phosphorescence (Yuan et al., 2017). Fluorescence occurs when atoms excited by light then the electrons transfer from ground state to first or second singlet state. After some time, the electrons release the absorbed energy and go back to the original ground state where the released energy is called fluorescence (Bao, Liu, Li, Qi & Sun, 2023) as described in diagram of Jablonski energy level in Fig. 3 a. Fluorescence remains for shorter time than phosphorescence after light absorption and excitation or shorter life time of decaying since the transition electrons to the excited singlet state are returned directly to the ground state after emitting photons (Krysmann, Kelarakis, Dallas & Giannelis, 2012).

The PL emission spectra depend on the wavelength of excitation where PL emission wavelength may be red shifted to increased wavelength that is higher than 400 nm that is called bathochromic shift or may be blue shifted to decreased wavelength that is lower than 400 nm that called hypochromic shift as in Fig. 3b. CQDs have emission color in range of blue-yellow color also, green or blue emission color has higher PL intensity which is reported in many articles (Krysmann et al., 2012; Liu et al., 2018; Zhang et al., 2022).
The mechanism of fluorescence is explained depending on three ways as CQDs surface state, molecular state and CQDs size effect (Song et al., 2015).

CQDs surface state depends on the resulted defects due to unperfected sp2 hybridization, presented functional groups and doping of heteroatom in carbon skeleton which result in increasing electron transitions with lower energy and longer wavelength causing bathochromic shift or red shift emission (Ding, Yu, Wei & Xiong, 2016). The fluorescence center is initiated from hybridization of carbon core and chemical groups. It is found that the carbonyl and carboxylic groups (withdrawing groups) to exhibit green color of emission as these groups withdraw electrons to make the carbon skeleton with lower electron density which means lower band gap that requires lower excitation energy (Fu et al., 2015). So, CQDs emit higher wavelength or red shift while hydroxyl groups (donating groups) to exhibit blue color of emission as such groups donate electrons toward the carbon skeleton that increase the electron density which means higher band gap that require higher excitation energy so, CQDs emit lower wavelength or blue shift (Liu et al., 2018; Sun, Li, Wu, Wei & Zeng, 2018).

In molecular state, the fluorescence center is initiated from organic fluorophore of the utilized carbon precursors which are fluorescent material where fluorophore has aromatic structures with many π bonds that re-emit light energy after light excitation (Song et al., 2015). Fluorescent molecules may be formed during CQDs synthesis which may be attached or unattached to CQDs surface that result in strong emission properties (Li et al., 2010; Moniruzzaman, Lakshmi, Kim & Kim, 2020).

By increasing size of CQDs, the band gap between energy levels decrease that require low excitation energy frequency which results in bathochromic shift or in other meaning; red shift. PL depends on CQDs size when it is less than the radius of their excitation where the excitation means the distance between the electron and its positive hole in the valence state (Eda et al., 2009; Shi, Nachtigallova, Aquino, Machado & Lischka, 2019). It is found that, CQDs with size of 1.2 nm showed to emit ultraviolet PL, CQDs with 1.5 to 3 nm sizes emit visible PL, and CQDs with 3.8 nm sizes emit near infra-red PL (Zhang et al., 2022).

Phosphorescence that has longer emission time than fluorescence, it was reported that, it could be initiated when CQDs are dissolved in polyvinyl alcohol then excited with UV light where in phosphorescence, the excited electrons are transferred from excited singlet state to triplet state then are returned to ground state. Also, the triplet states can be existed by carbonyl group which cause phosphorescence in addition to that, polyvinyl alcohol bond with carbonyl group by hydrogen bond preventing losing of triplet states. CQDs with phosphorescence remaining 9 s were synthesized and by increasing pH value, the PL decreased due to degradation of hydrogen bond leading to PL quenching ((Lu, Su & Yang, 2019; Zhang et al., 2022)).

In many searches, it is mentioned that CQDs have very low toxicity where the toxicity to cells is measured through vitro method of some specific cells. CQDs with QY of 21.7 % and size of 4.2 nm were synthesized from urea and cellulose via hydrothermal technique. The synthesized CQDs were examined for cytotoxicity where it was found that the MC3T3 cells were still remained alive by 89 % with different five concentrations of the synthesized CQDs (Shen et al., 2016).

CQDs with QY of 11.5 % were synthesized from chitosan and hydrochloric acid via microwave technique. The cytotoxicity of these CQDs were measured where it was found that the viability of the tested cells was remained at 94 % for different twelve CQDs concentrations (Janus, Piątkowski, Radwan-Pragłowska, Bogdał & Matysek, 2019). CQDs with size ranged from 5 to 10 nm and QY of 12.7 % synthesized from sodium alginate, hydrogen peroxide and water were utilized to measure cytotoxicity via MTT bioassay (Zhou et al., 2016). CQDs with size ranged from 1 to 3.4 nm and QY of 30.3 % were utilized to measure cytotoxicity (Liu et al., 2020). CQDs were synthesized with size of 5.3 nm from citric acid, ethylenediamine and deionized water via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs were shown with non-cytotoxic effects at concentration reached to 1 mg/mL (Liu et al., 2022).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Heterocyclic compounds as precursors for CQDs
ce:section.ce:para: Heterocyclic compounds are carbonic compounds that include heteroatoms as nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur, etc. which give them high importance in medical applications (Mermer, Keles & Sirin, 2021). Heterocyclic compounds can be obtained from natural and synthetic origins. Natural origins are plants and animals. Various parts of the plant can contain many heterocyclic compounds as alkaloids like caffeine, flavonoids, vitamins like vitamin B9 or folic acid, terpenoids and catechins (Quraishi, Chauhan & Saji, 2021; Verma, Ebenso, Bahadur & Quraishi, 2018). It is known that natural honey has several important compounds as fructose, glucose, sucrose, proteins, amino acids, vitamin C, flavonoids and other compounds. CQDs were synthesized from carbon source like honey, sulfur source like garlic and nitrogen source like ammonia via hydrothermal technique where the resulted CQDs were proved to be applied in therapeutic applications as described in Table 3 .

On the other hand, there are large diverse types of heterocyclic synthesized compounds that are utilized in CQDs synthesis as summarized in Table 4 . By hydrothermal technique, CQDs were synthesized from polyimide with QY of 20.9 % and size of 4 nm (Huang, Yang, Chen & Chen, 2015). Also, CQDs with size ranged from 5 to 10 nm were synthesized from azido-imidizole in ethanol solvent where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in Ag+ and Hg2+ detection (Wu, Feng, Chen & Tang, 2016). Waste paper contains cellulose, hemicellulose and lignin that are considered as heterocyclic compounds, were utilized in CQDs synthesis within nitric acid solvent via solvothermal technique where the formed CQDs had QY of 5.1 % and size 4.8 nm (Devi, Gupta, Paul & Tyagi, 2018). Poly ethylene glycol and papain were utilized in CQDs synthesis within ultrapure water via hydrothermal method where the synthesized CQDs had QY of 9.45 % and size ranged from 2 to 3 nm (Feng, Tan, Tang, Zhong & Yang, 2018).

In another report, heterocyclic compound as 1,7′-Dimethyl-2′-propyl-1H,3′H-[2,5]bibenzoimidazolyl was utilized for CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 2 to 3 nm via hydrothermal method for anticancer activity (Prasad et al., 2019). In another research, CQDs with size of 5.5 nm were synthesized from ampicillin via hydrothermal method for inactivation of bacteria (Gao, Yang, Wan & Yang, 2020). Also, folic acid, ethanol and cupric chloride were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 3.57 nm via solvothermal method to be applied for ascorbic acid sensing (Liu et al., 2020).

Recently, heterocyclic compounds that contain nitrogen atom as pyridine and pyrimidine were found to have promising significance. Firstly, Pyridine is important six-membered aromatic ring containing nitrogen atom that is utilized in drugs synthesis because of their promising properties like water solubility, basicity, stability, ability to form hydrogen bond, and also due to small molecular size (Hamada, 2018). There is high importance of compounds of pyridine and its derivatives (Kajita et al., 2022). CQDs were synthesized from pyridine via solution plasma method for determination of 2,4,6-trinitrophenol compound but this synthetic method is cost consumable and requires complicated equipment (Kim et al., 2020). Also, in comparative study; CQDs were prepared from pyridine and benzene in separate form via irradiation by femtosecond laser method. The CQDs that synthesized from pyridine have better optical characteristics than CQDs that synthesized from benzene because of nitrogen doped in pyridine (Astafiev et al., 2022). In another study, CQDs have been manufactured from pyridine derivatives like 3-aminoisonicotinic acid through hydrothermal method for detection of tryptophan in living cells and samples of human serum (Li et al., 2020). Also, CQDs were prepared from nicotinamide and α-hydroxy acids (Mao et al., 2022).

On the other hand, heterocyclic compounds that contain pyrimidine have high importance in medical applications for example; derivatives of 4-oxo-6-substituted phenyl-2-thioxo1,2,3,4-tetrahydropyrimidine-5-carbonitrile were prepared and utilized in inhibition of Human Immunodeficiency Virus integrase strand transfer (Wadhwa, Jain & Jadhav, 2021). Also, 6-(1,3-diphenyl- 1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-4-oxo-2-thioxo-1,2,3,4- tetrahydropyrimidine-5-carbonitrile was prepared and used in formation of antimicrobial and antitumor products (Ramadan, El-Helw & Sallam, 2019). Also, derivatives of 4-oxo-2-thioxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydropyrimidine-5-carbonitrile were synthesized and approved that having anticancer activity (Ghorab, El-Gazzar & Alsaid, 2014). Derivatives of 4-oxo-6-aryl-2-thioxo-1,2,3,4-tetrahydropyrimidine-5-carbonitrile were synthesized by imidazolium cation catalyst (Hasanpour, Eshghi & Mirzaei, 2017). CQDs utilized in formation of cotton textiles could be synthesized from diverse sources like prepared heterocyclic compounds such as 4–(2,4–dichlorophenyl)–6–oxo–2–thioxohexahydropyrimidine–5–carbonitrile (Emam et al., 2021). There is an interest in finding an easier technique to synthesis of CQDs from heterocyclic compounds. Eventually, it could be decided that, although different types of heterocyclic compounds were successfully exploited for synthesis of CQDs, but, according to all-the previously mentioned studies, CQDs ingrained from different heterocyclic compounds were exhibited with low quantum yield.

Carbohydrates exist in nature in plenty amounts with cheap price and diverse types where they are biomolecules with low toxicity and great medical importance. Carbohydrates have many features like low cost, high solubility in water, low required temperature for CQDs synthesis and low toxicity therefore; carbohydrates become interested precursors for CQDs synthesis. The presence of functional groups like hydroxyl, carboxylic groups and conjugation within the rings encourages for utilizing of them for CQDs synthesis. Carbohydrates can be divided to three categories like monosaccharide, disaccharides and polysaccharides according to their structural units’ number as in Fig. 4 . They were utilized in CQDs synthesis with different synthetic methods and solvents as following:

Fructose is simple sugar consisting of six membered atoms with ketone group. Fructose was utilized for synthesis of CQDs with size of 3.5 nm through dissolving in sodium hydroxide solution with heating (Li, Zhong, Rider, Furman & Ostrikov, 2014). Also, CQDs with size of 2.5 nm were synthesized from fructose with hydrochloric acid, polyethylene glycol, ethanol and sodium hydroxide via ultrasound irradiation (Costas-Mora, Romero, Lavilla & Bendicho, 2014). By another method, CQDs were synthesized from fructose by hydrothermal method (Cailotto et al., 2018). Ammonium bicarbonate and fructose were utilized in synthesis of CQDs via microwavable method (Wang et al., 2019). Also, CQDs with size ranged from 2 to 4 nm were synthesized from fructose via solvothermal method for sensing purposes (Liu, Zhang, Gao, Fei & Zhang, 2021). CQDs with size of 1.8 nm at pH = 5 were synthesized from fructose via solution plasma method (Treepet, Chokradjaroen, Kim, Saito & Watthanaphanit, 2022). Fructose was utilized for CQDs synthesis through three minutes in microwave within sulfuric acid solvent (He, Li, Feng & Long, 2022). There is an interested search for more simple and easy synthetic method of CQDs.

Glucose is simple sugar consisting of six membered atoms with aldehyde group. Glucose and its derivatives were utilized in CQDs synthesis in different ways like neutralization between acidic and basic mediums of ethylenediamine, glucose and nitric acid which resulted in CQDs with size 3 nm in addition to applications of sensing (Du et al., 2020). CQDs were synthesized from sodium hydroxide and glucose through ultrasonic method that resulted in QY of 7 % (Li et al., 2016). Also, CQDs with QY of 11 % and size of 3 nm were synthesized via hydrothermal method from taurine and glucose within distilled water for sensing purposes (Xu, Yan, Yue & Yu, 2019). In another research, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and glucose were utilized in CQDs synthesis with 4.5 nm via hydrothermal method for sensing purposes (Yuan, Chen, Cai et al., 2020). Also, CQDs with size of 2.45 nm were synthesized from aspartic acid and glucose within sodium hydroxide and water solvents via solvothermal method for sensing applications (Yuan, Chen, Zhou et al., 2020).

CQDs were synthesized from glucosamine within water and ethylenediamine solvents via microwavable method where the resulted CQDs had QY of 25.38 % and size of 4.45 nm (Tammina & Yang, 2020). In addition to that, CQDs were separately synthesized from glucose within urea, sodium persulfate, boric acid and deionized water solvents with size of 5.6, 6.9, 6.2 and 8.9 nm respectively via solvothermal / hydrothermal methods (Ezati, Rhim et al., 2022). CQDs were synthesized with size of 5.6 nm at pH 5 from glucose via solution plasma method (Treepet et al., 2022). Glucose was utilized for CQDs synthesis through three minutes in microwave within sulfuric acid solvent (He, Li et al., 2022). There is an interested search for more simple and easy carbon source for CQDs synthesis.

There are other monosaccharaides which are utilized in CQDs synthesis like mannose where mannose is aldohexose carbohydrates. There are little researches about mannose in CQDs synthesis where CQDs with size of 3.1 nm and QY of 9.8 % were synthesized from mannose and ammonium citrate via dry heating technique (Weng et al., 2015). Also, mannose was utilized in CQDs synthesis for antitumor applications (Zhou et al., 2021). There is an interested search for more simple and easy synthetic method of CQDs. Also, Xylose consists of one aldopentose unit. There are little articles that utilized xylose as carbon precursor where xylose was utilized in CQDs synthesis with 8.43 nm via microwavable method (Yang et al., 2020). Also, xylose was utilized for CQDs synthesis with size 2.72 nm via hydrothermal technique (Liao et al., 2020). So, it is important for searching about another natural carbon precursor that exist in plenty amount and give better results for CQDs synthesis.

Disaccharides consist of two saccharide units linked by glycosidic bond like lactose, maltose and sucrose in addition to their derivatives. Lactose is considered as disaccharide carbohydrates consisting of linked glucose and galactose units. CQDs with size ranged from 2 to 4 nm were synthesized from lactose via solvothermal method for Hg (II) detection (Liu et al., 2021). Lactose was utilized for CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 7 to 8 nm within hydrochloric acid solvent via solvothermal method (Houdová et al., 2021). Also, CQDs with size of 3.8 nm were synthesized from lactose within cysteine and sodium hydroxide solution via heating for five minutes at ninety degree centigrade via hotplate for sensing applications (Ngeontae et al., 2022). There is an interested search for more simple and easy synthetic method of CQDs.

Also, maltose is disaccharide carbohydrates consisting of two linked glucose units. CQDs were synthesized from maltose through sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide solutions separately (Li et al., 2014). Also, CQDs with size ranged from 2 to 4 nm were synthesized from maltose via solvothermal method for sensing purposes (Liu et al., 2021). There is continuous interested search for better natural carbon source to give better results for CQDs synthesis and applications.

Sucrose is disaccharide that consists of linked fructose and glucose units. It was reported that CQDs were synthesized by refluxing of sucrose within sulfuric acid and nitric acid (Hill & Galan, 2017). Sucrose was utilized for synthesis of CQDs with size 2 nm through ammonium hydroxide, phosphoric acid and water solvent via solvothermal method (Long et al., 2020). Also, CQDs were synthesized from sucrose within diethylene glycol in microwave oven for 1 min (Zhang et al., 2022).

Polysaccharides consist of more than two saccharide units linked by glycosidic bond in branched or linear shape. Polysaccharides are considered as one of the most important biological molecules where they represent one of the main sources for food and energy to most living organisms. There are two types of polysaccharides: homopolysaccharides and heteropolysaccharides (Majhi & Yadav, 2021). There is many advantages of utilizing of polysaccharides as precursors for CQDs synthesis as wide availability, nontoxicity, eco-friendly, cost-effective and high value of medical significance. Polysaccharides are polymeric building units of monosaccharaides that are bonded by glycosidic links. Also, polysaccharides can be easily broken down to small units by hydrolysis and easily carbonized for CQDs synthesis (Emam, 2022). There are diverse types of polysaccharides as chitin, chitosan, starch, cotton, viscose, cyclodexrins, xanthan, cellulose, lyocell, pectin, sodium alginate (Emam & Ahmed, 2016).

Alginate is considered as linear polysaccharides that extracted from algae where alginate consists of β-D-manuronic acid and α-L-guluronic acid that contain hydroxyl and carboxyl groups resulted in many applications. sodium alginate, hydrogen peroxide and water were utilized in CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 5 to 10 nm and QY of 12.6 % via solvothermal method for bioimaging and medical applications (Zhou et al., 2016). CQDs with size ranged from 2 to 5 nm were synthesized from sodium alginate, hydrochloric acid and glutaraldehyde via solvothermal technique (Sun et al., 2020). CQDs were synthesized with QY of 50.2 % and size of 2.8 nm from melatonin and sodium alginate via pyrolysis for five hours in autoclave unit at 180° centigrade for biosensing purposes (Xu, Tang, Yu & Ge, 2021). Also, CQDs were synthesized with size ranged from 3 to 4 nm from glutathione, formamide and sodium alginate via solvothermal with microwave technique for sensing purposes and cell imaging (Liang et al., 2022).

Chitin is considered as polysaccharide that consists of N-acetyl glucosamine where chitin is second in abundance after cellulose which provides a big chance for many uses and applications. Chitin and nitric acid were propelled to synthesis CQDs with size ranged from 4 to 8 nm via hydrothermal technique (Shchipunov, Khlebnikov & Silant'Ev, 2015). CQDs with average size of 6 nm and QY of 5.1 % were synthesized via microwavable technique from chitin nanofibers, calcium chloride and ethanol for sensing purposes (Naghdi, Atashi, Golmohammadi, Saeedi & Alanezhad, 2017). CQDs with QY of 6 % were synthesized from chitin within urea and potassium hydroxide via hydrothermal technique (Su, Wang & Yan, 2019). CQDs were synthesized with QY of 25.8 % and size of 4.21 nm from chitin, deionized water and ammonia for sensing purposes (Jiang, Ni, Gao & Zhou, 2020). Also, CQDs were synthesized from chitin within sulfonic acid and quaternary ammonium salt via solvothermal method for sensing purposes (M. Feng, Wang, He, Chen & Sun, 2022).

Pectin is considered as polysaccharide consisting of galacturonic acid. Pectin and sodium hydroxide were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 2.7 nm and QY of 1.1 % via hydrothermal technique for two hours at different temperatures(Zhao, Zhang & Zhou, 2014). Also, Pectin and sodium hydroxide were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 4 nm and QY of 5.38 % via hydrothermal technique for one hour after stirring for 30 min for sensing of Ag+(Ahmed & Veerappan, 2016).

Cellulose is considered as polysaccharide consisting of glucose units where cellulose is the most widely available on earth. CQDs with QY of 21.7 % and size of 4.2 nm were synthesized from urea and cellulose via hydrothermal technique (Shen et al., 2016). Also, CQDs were synthesized from cellulose within ethylenediamine solvent via solvothermal technique where the resulted CQDs had 3.2 nm and QY of 55 % (Wu, Li, Wu, Liu & Liu, 2017). CQDs with QY of 32.3 % and size of 5.45 nm were synthesized from cellulose within basic solvents for different time intervals at various temperature degrees for sensing purposes (Liao et al., 2020). Also, CQDs with size ranged from 0.7 to 3.0 nm were synthesized from cellulose via hydrothermal technique for sensing purposes (Zattar, de Mesquita & Pereira, 2022).

Oxidized cellulose was utilized for CQDs synthesis with ammonia solvent via solvothermal method where the size of synthesized CQDs ranged from 1 to 3.4 nm and QY of 30.3 % for sensing purposes and medical applications (Liu et al., 2020). CQDs were synthesized with size of 4.7 nm and QY of 22.9 % from carboxymethylcellulose via hydrothermal method (Issa et al., 2020). CQDs were synthesized from carboxymethylcellulose with sodium hydroxide via infrared synthetic method where the resulted CQDs had size of 8 nm (Ahmed et al., 2021).

Chitosan consists of N-acetyl-D-glucosamine and D-glucosamine units. CQDs were synthesized with QY of 4.34 % and size ranged from 1 to 6 nm from chitosan through carbonization technique with atmosphere of nitrogen for medical purposes (Liu, Pang, Xu & Zhang, 2016). CQDs with size <10 nm and QY of 11.5 % were synthesized from chitosan and hydrochloric acid via microwavable technique for medical applications (Janus et al., 2019). CQDs with yield of 45.9 % were synthesized from chitosan and acrylamide within acetic acid solvent via microwave and hydrothermal methods for sensing purposes (Liu et al., 2019). carboxymethylchitosan as chitosan derivative was utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 7.3 nm through hydrochloric acid, water and ethylenediamine solvents via microwavable method for 2 min (Guo et al., 2020). Also, chitosan, water and hydrogen peroxide were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 2.6 to 3.4 nm via solvothermal method (Chen et al., 2020). There is interested search for more simple and easy synthetic method of CQDs.

Starch consists of linear amylose and branched amylopectin. CQDs with size of 3 nm were synthesized from starch while CQDs with size of 4.5 nm were synthesized from starch and ethylene diamine via microwavr technique (Zheng, Liu, Yang, Liu & Xu, 2018). Also, CQDs were synthesized with size of 4.2 nm and QY of 24.5 % from starch via hydrothermal technique for sensing purposes (Man et al., 2020). Phosphoric acid and starch were utilized for CQDs synthesis via microwave method (Shibata et al., 2022). In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from starch via both hydrothermal and microwave methods (Mary, Koshy, Arunima, Thomas & Pothen, 2022). CQDs were synthesized with QY of 49.26 % from starch and ethylenediamine via ultrasonication technique for sensing purposes (Preethi, Murugan, Viswanathan & Ponpandian, 2022).

Carrageenan is considered as polysaccharide that consist of galactose units where CQDs were synthesized from carrageenan with size 3.2 nm and QY of 14.64 % via hydrothermal technique for sensing applications (Das et al., 2019). CQDs with size of 2.1 nm were synthesized from carrageenan via hydrothermal technique for antiviral and antitumor purposes (Emam & Ahmed, 2021). Also, CQDs were synthesized with size of 8 nm from carrageenan, chitosan, acetic acid and ultrapure water via hydrothermal technique for sensing purposes (Ji et al., 2021). CQDs were synthesized from carrageenan and glutathione via hydrothermal technique for sensing purposes (Qi et al., 2022).

Arabic gum is branched polysaccharide that is extracted from branches and stems of acacia where CQDs with size of 7 nm were synthesized from arabic gum in addition to sodium hydroxide, ethanol and distilled water via microwavable technique for bio-imaging, drug delivery and anti-bacterial purposes (Thakur et al., 2014). Also, CQDs with size of 9 nm were synthesized from gum via microwavable method (Pires et al., 2015). Arabic gum was utilized in CQDs synthesis with size ranged from 1.5 to 6.5 nm via hydrothermal method (Ahmed & Emam, 2020). CQDs with size of 4.8 nm were synthesized from extract of gum via microwavable technique for bioimaging applications as diagnostic of cancer cells (Sekar, Yadav, Kannaiyan & Munuswamy-Ramanujam, 2021). There is continuous search for more simple and easy synthetic method of CQDs. Also, Pullulan is considered as polysaccharide consisting of many maltotriose units. CQDs with size of 2.1 nm were synthesized from pullulan via hydrothermal technique for antiviral and antitumor purposes (Emam & Ahmed, 2021).

Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) as unique member in the family of optical active, biocompatible and nontoxic nanomaterials, to be excellently applicable as alternative for the metallic nanostructures and organic coloring reagents in various purposes such as, electronics, chemical or bio-sensing, and biomedical fields (Baker & Baker, 2010; Hola et al., 2014; Kozak et al., 2016; Zheng, Ananthanarayanan, Luo & Chen, 2015). Whereas, CQDs are advantageous with superior characters, like, resistant to photo-bleaching, cost effectiveness and simple synthetic techniques and easy for bio-conjugation (Wang et al., 2017). Moreover, CQDs were reported to be considerably applicable in different environmental purposes, such as water treatment (Majeed et al., 2024). However, the exploitation of CQDs in different biomedical purposes is found to be much more promisingly considered, owing to its non-cytotoxic impacts (Baker & Baker, 2010; Hola et al., 2014; Kozak et al., 2016; Wang et al., 2017; Zheng et al., 2015).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Applications of CQDs in medical purposes
ce:section.ce:para: In recent years, diverse medical applications of CQDs were appeared due to the biocompatibility properties of CQDs. It is found that, CQDs can't reach to the brain where CQDs can't penetrate the blood barrier of brain while they can reach to the remaining body organs like liver, spleen and kidney. Also, it is found that, CQDs were extracted from the body through 4.2 h while CQDs can disperse in the body through 12 min. CQDs were known that they have low cytotoxicity where cytotoxicity means death of living cells because of affecting by toxic chemical structures. In addition to the several advantages of CQDs like low cost, high availability of carbon precursors, high photostability, good biocompatibility, good chemical inertness (Emam, 2022). CQDs have many medical purposes as in Fig. 5 .

Due to the biocompatibility and photoluminescence of CQDs, they can be used in imaging of specific cells in the body of animal or human where CQDs have limited emission wavelengths depending on excitation wavelengths. There are several searches that utilized CQDs in bioimaging like CQDs with size of 2.7 nm and QY of 1.1 % that were synthesized from pectin and sodium hydroxide, are utilized for bioimaging of HEp-2 cells (Zhao et al., 2014). CQDs with size of 7 nm were synthesized from arabic gum in addition to sodium hydroxide, ethanol and distilled water via microwavable technique for bioimaging purposes (Thakur et al., 2014). Also, CQDs with QY of 21.7 % and size of 4.2 nm were synthesized from urea and cellulose via hydrothermal technique. The synthesized CQDs were examined for bioimaging where MC3T3 cells displayed green and blue emissions with excitation at 488 and 405 nm respectively (Shen et al., 2016).

In another article, 3-Bromophenol was utilized in CQDs synthesis within deionized water and ethanol solvents via carbonization method that resulted in QY of 19.6 % and size of 5.2 nm where the resulted CQDs were applied for bioimaging (Zou et al., 2016). CQDs were synthesized with size ranged from 1 to 6 nm from chitosan through carbonization technique with atmosphere of nitrogen. The synthesized CQDs were examined for bioimaging where root cells of onion were excited at 405 nm then emitted green color showing the cell membrane and nuclei (Liu et al., 2016). CQDs with size ranged from 5 to 10 nm and QY of 12.7 % were synthesized from sodium alginate, hydrogen peroxide and water for bioimaging applications (Zhou et al., 2016).

Due to the several advantages of CQDs as good biocompatibility, low toxicity, having fluorescence with high intensity and water solubility, CQDs are utilized in biological imaging. CQDs are utilized in bioactive molecules detection where CQDs doped with heteroatom like silicon or boron can detect hydrogen peroxide since hydrogen peroxide react with fluorescent CQDs that result in quenching of CQDs fluorescence. also, CQDs are utilized in detection of glucose by forming covalent bond between CQDs and glucose (Feng & Qian, 2018). In other research, synthesized CQDs with surface modification were utilized in H1299 cells bioimaging via hypochlorite as fluorescent probes where hypochlorite is used through water purification as disinfectant. It was found that hypochlorite changed the emission wavelength of the synthesized CQDs with surface modification from 413 to 508 nm which resulted in color change from blue to green color. This color change was seen through H1299 cells images as in Fig. 6 . The CQDs were utilized in mouse imaging (Yang et al., 2019), while CQDs with size ranged from 1 to 3.4 nm and QY of 30.3 % were utilized to measure bioimaging (Liu et al., 2020).

In addition to that, CQDs with size of 3.3 nm and QY of 47.1 % were synthesized from procaine, hydrochloride, citric acid, ethylenediamine and distilled water via solvothermal method where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in cell imaging (Zhao et al., 2020). In another research, CQDs with size ranged from 6 to 8 nm and QY of 9 % were synthesized from imidazole, poly ethylene glycol, phosphoric acid and ammonia via thermal technique where the synthesized CQDs displayed bioimaging property of cells (Bajpai et al., 2020). CQDs with QY of 28.2 % and size of 5 nm were synthesized from leaves of Catharanthus roseus via hydrothermal technique then CQDs were utilized in bioimaging (Arumugham, Alagumuthu, Amimodu, Munusamy & Iyer, 2020). CQDs with QY of 2.5 % and size of 4.9 nm were synthesized from cyclodextrin and methylene blue via electric oven where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in bioimaging (Wen, Zhao, Zhai, Wang & Li, 2020). In addition to that, CQDs with size of 3.9 nm were synthesized from glycol and glucose via hydrothermal technique for cell imaging applications (Duan et al., 2020). Also, folic acid, ethanol and cupric chloride were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 3.57 nm via solvothermal method to be applied for imaging of cells (Liu et al., 2020).

Urea, tartaric acid and dimethyl formamide were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size 8.20 nm for imaging of cell (Sun et al., 2020). O-phenylenediamine and selenourea were utilized in CQDs synthesis in hydrochloric acid solvent via solvothermal method with QY of 23.6 % and size of 3 nm for malachite detection in tissue of fish (Hu et al., 2021). In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from nitrobenzaldehyde and glutaraldehyde in separate form within ethanol solvent via solvothermal method that resulted in size of 5 and 1 nm respectively for bioimaging purposes (Mohammadi et al., 2021). CQDs with size of 4.8 nm were synthesized from extract of gum via microwavable technique for bioimaging applications as diagnostic of cancer cells (Sekar et al., 2021). Also, CQDs were synthesized with size ranged from 3 to 4 nm from glutathione, formamide and sodium alginate via solvothermal with microwave technique for sensing purposes and cell imaging (Liang et al., 2022). Also, CQDs were synthesized via microwavable method for bioimaging purposes (Tejwan et al., 2022). CQDs were synthesized with size of 5.3 nm from citric acid, ethylenediamine and deionized water via hydrothermal technique for fungal imaging (Liu et al., 2022).

Because of the very small size of CQDs that is below ten nanometers which leads to high surface area, CQDs can be utilized as nano carrier for drugs and gene that facilitate the arriving of drugs or gene to targeted cells. There are many researches that utilized CQDs as nano carriers such as CQDs with size of 7 nm that were synthesized from arabic gum in addition to sodium hydroxide, ethanol and distilled water via microwavable technique for drug delivery purposes (Thakur et al., 2014). CQDs with size ranged from 5 to 10 nm and QY of 12.7 % were synthesized from sodium alginate, hydrogen peroxide and water that utilized for gene delivery (Zhou et al., 2016). Also, CQDs with size of 5.2 nm and QY of 76.12 % were synthesized from folic acid, carrageenan and distilled water via hydrothermal technique. The resulted CQDs were utilized as nano carrier of capecitabine which is anticancer drug (Das et al., 2019).

In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from m-phenylenediamine via hydrothermal technique with QY of 12 % and size of 5.1 nm where the synthesized CQDs were utilized as drug carriers (Saravanan et al., 2020). In another research, CQDs with size of 3.9 nm were synthesized from glycol and glucose via hydrothermal technique for drug delivery applications (Duan et al., 2020). Also, CQDs were synthesized via microwavable method for drug delivery purposes (Tejwan et al., 2022). The mechanism of using CQDs in drug delivery was proposed as following: the cancer drug is loaded through the synthesized CQDs then the CQDs loaded with cancer drug attack the cancer cell causing penetration of cancer drug to the cancer cell nucleus that leads to death of the cancer cell (Emam, 2022) as described in Fig. 7 .

Due to the disadvantages of using chemotherapy for treating cancer like nonspecific targeting cancer cells and resistance of the body toward the drugs, there is continuous search for targeting drugs. The current chemotherapy drugs spreads in the blood path to all the body that the chemotherapy drugs can harm the healthy cells which causes hair loss and other syndromes. There is continuous research for new anticancer drugs that target the cancer cells without toxic effect on good cells (Jana & Dev, 2022). Due to the quite tiny size of CQDs and high surface area, CQDs can penetrate the cell membrane and enter to the tumor cell cytoplasm then CQDs can stimulate reactive oxygen species generation. Reactive oxygen species reach to the nucleus and DNA that result in damaging them which leads to the death of the tumor cells.

For example, CQDs with size of 3.3 nm and QY of 47.1 % were synthesized from procaine, hydrochloride, citric acid, ethylenediamine and distilled water via solvothermal method. The synthesized CQDs were utilized in inhibiting of tumor of DLD1 (Zhao et al., 2020). Also, CQDs with QY of 42.7 % and size of 2 nm were synthesized from ethylenediamine and citric acid via refluxing technique. The synthesized CQDs were utilized in generation of reactive oxygen species for anticancer reactivity in addition to that, CQDs can be extracted from the body within urine (Pang et al., 2020).

CQDs with size of 8 nm were synthesized from mushroom, sulfuric acid and deionized water via solvothermal method where the resulted CQDs displayed anticancer property for cells of breast cancer that is due to the binding of CQDs to DNA of cells of breast cancer (Boobalan et al., 2020). In another research, CQDs with size ranged from 6 to 8 nm and QY of 9 % were synthesized from imidazole, poly ethylene glycol, phosphoric acid and ammonia via thermal technique. The synthesized CQDs displayed anticancer property for melanoma cells (Bajpai et al., 2020). Also, CQDs with size of 5.2 nm and QY of 76.12 % were synthesized from folic acid, carrageenan and distilled water via hydrothermal technique.

The resulted CQDs were utilized as nano carrier of capecitabine which is anticancer drug (Das et al., 2019). Also, CQDs with QY of 94.5 % and size of 5.4 nm were synthesized from folic acid via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in anticancer reactivity and imaging of cancer cells (Liu et al., 2018). CQDs were synthesized with size ranged from 2 to 3 nm via hydrothermal method from derivative of bi-benzoimidazolyl for anticancer reactivity to cells of breast cancer (Prasad et al., 2019). CQDs with QY of 28.2 % and size of 5 nm were synthesized from leaves of Catharanthus roseus via hydrothermal technique then the resulted CQDs were utilized in anticancer reactivity for cancer cells of breast MCF-7 (Arumugham et al., 2020). CQDs with QY of 2.5 % and size of 4.9 nm were synthesized from cyclodextrin and methylene blue via electric oven.

The synthesized CQDs were utilized in anticancer reactivity where CQDs displayed inhibition of growth of cells of cancer (Wen et al., 2020). In addition to that, CQDs with size of 3.9 nm were synthesized from glycol and glucose via hydrothermal technique for drug delivery applications (Duan et al., 2020). CQDs with size of 2.1 nm were synthesized from carrageenan via hydrothermal technique for antitumor purposes (Emam & Ahmed, 2021). In addition to that, CQDs were utilized in tumor diagnosis (Wang et al., 2024). The mechanism of anticancer reactivity of synthesized CQDs was proposed that CQDs resulted reactive species that can penetrate the mitochondria of the cancer cell causing cancer cell apoptosis (Emam, 2024) as described in Fig. 8 a.

It is found that, CQDs have antibacterial activity where due to the large surface area of CQDs, they can penetrate the bacterial cell and reach to the nucleus causing stimulation of reactive oxygen species that causing bacterial cell death. CQDs with size of 7 nm were synthesized from arabic gum in addition to sodium hydroxide, ethanol and distilled water via microwavable technique for anti-bacterial purposes (Thakur et al., 2014). CQDs with size of 8 nm were synthesized from mushroom, sulfuric acid and deionized water via solvothermal method where the resulted CQDs displayed antibacterial property for three types of bacteria (Boobalan et al., 2020). In addition to that, CQDs were synthesized from m-phenylenediamine via hydrothermal technique with QY of 12 % and size of 5.1 nm where the synthesized CQDs were utilized as antimicrobial reagents (Saravanan et al., 2020). In another research, CQDs with size of 5.5 nm were synthesized from ampicillin via hydrothermal method for inactivation of bacteria (Gao et al., 2020a).

In addition to that; 1,5-diaminonaphthalene, citric acid and ethanol solvent were utilized for CQDs synthesis with size of 9.6 nm for antibacterial purposes (Nie et al., 2020). Also, CQDs with size of 7 nm were synthesized from Levofloxacin via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs showed antibacterial activity in both vitro and vivo methods (Wu et al., 2022). In addition to that, CQDs with size of 1.82 nm were synthesized from onion via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs showed damaging of bacterial cells (Lin, Cheng & Tan, 2022). Also, CQDs with average size of 8.5 nm were synthesized from turmeric, ammonium persulfate and deionized water at 200° centigrade via hydrothermal technique for 6 h. The synthesized CQDs were put on films of gelatin and pectin where it displayed antibacterial action (Ezati, Roy & Rhim, 2022). In another article, CQDs synthesized from tea residues and/or petroleum coke with size of 4.5 and 6 nm respectively via chemical oxidation method, were found to have anti-bacterial performance contra both of Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacteria through inhibiting bacteria strains growth with high diameter of inhibition zone (Saikia et al., 2023). The mechanism of antibacterial performance of synthesized CQDs was proposed that CQDs are adhered on bacterial cell surface that results in destruction of cell wall of the bacterial cell which permits penetration of reactive species causing death of the bacterial cell (Emam, 2024) as explained in Fig. 8b.

It is found that, CQDs have antiviral activity where CQDs inhibit binding of virus cells to the body cells and inhibit replication of DNA of virus cells. CQDs with size of 4.5 nm were synthesized from ascorbic acid and polyethylene glycol diamine for antiviral property (Du et al., 2016). Also, CQDs with QY of 40 % and size of 4.5 nm were synthesized via hydrothermal method for antiviral activity of Coronavirus (Łoczechin et al., 2019). In addition to that, CQDs with size of 1.5 nm and QY of 3.6 % were synthesized from citric acid and curcumin via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs can inhibit virus of porcine epidemic diarrhea that is similar to coronavirus (Ting et al., 2018). CQDs with size ranged from 4 to 5 nm were synthesized from curcumin via pyrolyzing at different temperatures to inhibit enterovirus 71 (Lin et al., 2019). CQDs with size of 4.4 nm were synthesized from sodium hydroxide and benzoxazine via hydrothermal technique where the synthesized CQDs can block the life cycle of virus (Huang et al., 2019). CQDs with size of 2.1 nm were synthesized from carrageenan via hydrothermal technique for antiviral purposes (Emam & Ahmed, 2021). In another research, CQDs were synthesized from chitosan linked with glucose that approved to have higher antiviral activity against Corona Virus than CQDs that synthesized from chitosan (Ahmed, El-Shahat, Allayeh & Emam, 2023). The proposed mechanism of antiviral activity of synthesized CQDs is that reactive species can be generated from the synthesized CQDs and then the reactive species react with viral protein and destroy phospholipase causing viral cell apoptosis, stopping genome replication and DNA inhibition (Emam, 2024) as illustrated in Fig. 8c.

Bone regeneration is a vital biological process that acts in maintaining the bone health and the treatment of the bone-related disordering. Despite of the challenges that are related to the bone regeneration, significant advances were investigated recently, and researchers are expected to result in the progression for more effective treating for various bone-related conditions. Bone regeneration is essential for the treatment of different bone-related disordering, such as bone fractures, osteoporosis, osteonecrosis, and bone tumors. It helps for restoring the structural integrity and functionality of the bone. Bone regeneration acts in enabling the repairing of the deteriorated bone tissues, which is essential for saving the bone health and stop further degeneration. Bone regeneration can inhibit the bone losing that is a common consequence of aging, trauma, or disease (Haider, Batool & Javeed, 2022).

CQDs were utilized in regeneration of new cells for injured bones or treating bone defects where by combination of specific cells as osteoblast, stem or fibroblast with calcium source as hydroxyapatite, growth factors and CQDs, the injured bones recovered. This is due to the bone consists of hydroxyapatite in addition to collagen (Emam, 2022). Also, CQDs with size of 4 nm were synthesized via hydrothermal technique from seeds of Nigella Sativa where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in modification of scaffolds polycaprolactone by enhancing the hydrophilicity of polycaprolactone for engineering of tissue (Ehtesabi & Massah, 2021). In addition to that, CQDs were utilized in bone and cartilage repair, myocardium regeneration or cardiac tissue engineering, neural regeneration and wound healing ((Singh et al., 2024; Zong, Chen, Zhao, Wang & Wu, 2024)).
