The mechanism of utilization of CQDs in engineering of bone tissue is described in Fig. 9 (Emam, 2022).

CQDs were proved to have anti-inflammation action in several researches where inflammation can be described as natural re-action of the organism to any dangerous external attacks as toxic pathogens or chemicals which try to damage the cells. Inflammation may be simple or complicated where simple inflammation is lower effect and is associated with specific position of the body but when simple inflammation increases in effect and time, complicated inflammation occurred which leads to reactive nitrogen or oxygen species accumulation that hurt healthy cells causing diseases (Sharma, Choi & Lee, 2023). The attacked cells tend to produce nitric oxide (NO) that is considered as an indicator for inflammation presence. There are various articles that described the anti-inflammatory features of CQDs, for example, CQDs with size of 6.9 nm were synthesized from ibuprofen and tri-ethanol amine as carbon sprouts via irradiation of microwave where it was proved that the synthesized CQDs have anti-inflammatory features. Three inflamed mice groups via carrageenan were injected with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) as control group, ibuprofen and ibuprofen-CQDs (ICDs) respectively in separate form. The results displayed that the CDs samples had lower neutrophils level than the two other groups where lower neutrophils level is related to lower inflamed tissues that indicates to higher anti-inflammatory features of CDs as showed in Fig. 10 (Qu et al., 2020). In another research, CQDs were synthesized from genistein via hydrothermal mode where the resulted CQDs showed anti-inflammatory merits in vitro tests (Jaiswal et al., 2023).

CQDs can act as both oxidizing agents and antioxidizing agents according to the surrounding conditions. When CQDs accept ultraviolet or visible light, CQDs act as oxidizing agents which means generation of reactive species while when CQDs scavenge free radicals, they act as antioxidizing agents (Innocenzi & Stagi, 2023). Reactive species as nitrogen or oxygen species are hurtful to biological ingredients of cells as proteins, DNA and lipids. It is found CQDs have good protective effect of cells against oxidative damage which make CQDs to be utilized in medical purposes (Tang, Ding, Zhang, Cai & Bao, 2023). Scavenging of radicals is important to prevent oxidative stress in cells avoiding many illnesses. There is standard method for measuring efficacy of radicals scavenging which is DPPH or 2, 2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazil that is used with any sample (Blois, 1958). CQDs synthesized from tea residues and petroleum coke in separate form with size of 4.5 and 6 nm respectively via chemical oxidation method, were found to have activity of radicals scavenging that when CQDs solution mixed with DPPH solution, the functional groups of CQDs surface as hydroxyl and carboxyl groups react with DPPH for stabilizing DPPH molecules by deprotonating the CQDs surface functional groups. As a result of this, the CQDs surface functional groups generate free radicals which are stabilized via unpaired electrons resonance that make the fluorescence of CQDs remain constant (Saikia et al., 2023).

In addition to that, it was proven that CQDs were utilized in oxygen or nitrogen species scavenging. For example, CQDs were synthesized from genistein which is isoflavone from soybean beside ethyelene diamine and NaOH via solvo-thermal technique for 8 h at 180 °C. reactive oxygen species / reactive nitrogen species solutions were synthesized for measuring anti-oxidation reactivity where it was found that CQDs can reduce reactive species with high percent (Jaiswal et al., 2023). Also, CQDs with size of 2.39 nm that were synthesized from methylene violet via hydrothermal mode are proved to have antioxidant capacity. The oxidant and anti-oxidant activity of CQDs was obtained for CQDs were synthesized from tomato juice via hydrothermal mode. DPPH radicals are stabilized with protons of functional groups of CQDs surface that results in formation of radicals on CQDs surface which are stabilized via unpaired electrons delocalization through resonance of aromatic medium of CQDs core. Stabilization of CQDs surface may also be occurred via rearrangement of functional groups bonds of CQDs surface (Rodríguez-Varillas et al., 2022).

Because of excellent biocompatibility, high surface area, water solubility and good photobleaching resistance of CQDs, CQDs are utilized in biosensing purposes. Biosensing means detection of biological molecules as nucleic acids, glucose, etc. based on fluorescence features of CQDs. Nucleic acids consist of ribonucleic acid or RNA and deoxyribonucleic acid or DNA. DNA was detected through interaction of fluorescent CQDs with quencher like methylene blue which makes CQDs to be un-fluorescent then in presence of DNA, CQDs return to be fluorescent due to interaction of DNA with the quencher. In addition to that, CQDs were utilized in cancer cells detection where CQDs were functionalized with folic acid which makes it to be good nanoprobe. This nanoprobe had affinity to react with cancer cells folate receptor like HeLa cells (Feng & Qian, 2018).

Also, CQDs biosensors are utilized in detection of infectious diseases and non-infectious diseases via sensing of proteins, ions, chemical compounds as uric acid, vitamins as vitamin C and B, glucose and neoplasms (Barrientos et al., 2023). In addition to that, CQDs were applicable for cesium bio-sensing via fluorescence quenching through sea water sample where cesium is radioactive isotope (Chellasamy, Arumugasamy, Govindaraju & Yun, 2022). In other research, CQDs were synthesized and exploited in water sensing through organic solvents in addition to hypochlorite sensing (Zhu et al., 2023). In another research, CQDs with size of 3 nm were synthesized via microwave technique where the synthesized CQDs were utilized in breast cancer detection (Amir, Subramanian, Sornambikai, Ponpandian & Viswanathan, 2024).

Carbon quantum dots are special class of carbon nanostructures with spherical size of one to ten nanometers. Due to high availability of carbon precursors, biocompatibility, high surface area, low cost, high photo-stability and low cytotoxicity properties of carbon quantum dots, they have diverse medical applications which are currently summarized in the presented review. Synthesis techniques of carbon quantum dots are diverse from top-down to bottom-up techniques but most of them are complicated, energy consuming, time-consuming and expensive. However, the hydrothermal technique has advantages of high yield, easy control and simplicity than other synthesis methods. The current review represents an overview for the compatibility of Saccharides (e.g. glucose and lactose) and polysaccharides (e.g. pectin, alginate, cellulose, chitosan) as biogenic precursor versus heterocyclic compounds for successive clustering of carbon quantum dots. It could be depicted that, although different types of heterocyclic compounds were successfully exploited for synthesis of CQDs, but, according to all-the previously mentioned studies, CQDs ingrained from different heterocyclic compounds were exhibited with low quantum yield. On contrast, there is many advantages of utilizing of polysaccharides as precursors for CQDs synthesis as wide availability, nontoxicity, eco-friendly, cost-effective and high value of medical significance, in addition to, the previously studies in literature showed that, different polysaccharides could be successfully exploited in clustering of CQDs with higher quantum yield. So, it can be decided that, different polysaccharides showed higher compatibility and superiority for nucleation of CQDs to be promisingly applicable for different purposes such as anticancer, antiviral, antimicrobial, anti-oxidant, anti-inflammatory reagents, drug delivery and engineering of bone tissues.

Hanan B. Ahmed: Writing – original draft, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Data curation, Conceptualization. Mary M. Mikhail: Writing – original draft, Validation, Software, Methodology, Data curation, Conceptualization. Mahmoud El-Shahat: Validation, Supervision, Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization. Hossam E. Emam: Writing – review & editing, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Data curation, Conceptualization.
